Table of Contents

ITEM 1. BUSINESS ........ 5

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS ........ 12

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS ........ 45

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Sandisk Corporation designs, develops, and manufactures NAND flash storage solutions for datacenter, client, and consumer markets worldwide. Our products include SSDs, embedded storage, and removable cards used in enterprise servers, personal computers, mobile devices, and automotive applications. We operate manufacturing facilities and partner with foundries to deliver high-performance storage at scale. Our strategy focuses on technology leadership in 3D NAND, expanding datacenter share, and disciplined capital allocation. Competition remains intense across all end markets. We continue to invest in next-generation cell designs, controller firmware, and system-level integration to sustain performance leadership. Our go-to-market organization partners with OEMs, cloud providers, and channel distributors globally. Seasonal demand patterns and customer inventory cycles can create quarter-to-quarter volatility in shipments and average selling prices.

Our addressable market spans enterprise storage, client computing, mobile, automotive, and industrial applications. Each segment has distinct performance, endurance, and cost requirements that inform our product roadmap. We monitor industry bit growth, capital spending by competitors, and macroeconomic indicators that influence IT spending and consumer device purchases.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

Investing in our common stock involves substantial risks. Demand for NAND products is cyclical and sensitive to macroeconomic conditions, inventory levels at customers, and pricing trends. We face intense competition from other memory manufacturers that may have greater scale or resources. Supply chain disruptions, geopolitical tensions, or trade restrictions could adversely affect our ability to source equipment, materials, or sell products in key markets. Customer concentration among hyperscale buyers may increase pricing volatility and reduce our negotiating leverage.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

Revenue increased during fiscal 2025 driven by stronger datacenter demand and improved pricing. Gross margin expanded as mix shifted toward higher-value enterprise SSDs. Operating expenses rose modestly as we invested in R&D and go-to-market capabilities. Cash flow from operations remained positive, supporting capital expenditures for technology transitions. We expect continued volatility in NAND pricing and are focused on cost competitiveness and product differentiation.

ITEM 8. FINANCIAL STATEMENTS AND SUPPLEMENTARY DATA

The consolidated financial statements and notes thereto are set forth in Item 8 of this Annual Report on Form 10-K. Refer to the index to consolidated financial statements for a listing of all financial statement schedules and exhibits filed as part of this report. The auditor's report and balance sheets follow in subsequent pages of this filing.
